title: CSV `COPY TO` to S3 doesn’t respect FILE_SIZE_BYTES
description:
### What happens?

Hi DuckDB team 👋

First off, thanks for all your work on DuckDB and the surrounding ecosystem.

I ran into an issue where COPY ... TO with FORMAT CSV doesn’t respect FILE_SIZE_BYTES when writing to S3 via httpfs. There’s an existing #18381 issue tracking similar behavior without much details in the main repo (opened Jul 23, 2025, currently labeled “under review”).

What I’m seeing
When exporting CSV to S3 with FILE_SIZE_BYTES set, the export produces a single object with all rows instead of rolling over into multiple files.

Proposed fix (in duckdb-httpfs)
I opened a PR in duckdb/duckdb-httpfs (PR [#146](https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb-httpfs/pull/146)) that makes CSV COPY TO enforce FILE_SIZE_BYTES on S3. In short, I found that on the S3 path the writer always reported a length of 0, so the limit check never triggered. The change updates the length as data is written and adds a small test (based on the file_size_bytes test in the main repo).

Why I’m filing here
There’s an existing report in duckdb/duckdb (#18381). On Oct 9, 2025 I opened a matching PR in duckdb/duckdb-httpfs with a small fix, but as of Oct 20, 2025 it hasn’t received reviews or replies yet. I know everyone’s busy and duckdb-httpfs gets fewer eyes, so I’m opening this in the main repo just to surface the context and ask for guidance on the best path forward. 

### To Reproduce


```
CREATE TABLE bigdata AS SELECT i AS col_a, i AS col_b FROM range(0,10000) tbl(i);

COPY (FROM bigdata) TO 's3://test-bucket/file_size_bytes_csv1' (FORMAT CSV, FILE_SIZE_BYTES 1000);

---should lead to 3 files
SELECT count(*) FROM glob('s3://test-bucket/file_size_bytes_csv1/*.csv')
```

### OS:

MacOS arm64

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.1

### DuckDB Client:

CLI / Python / etc

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Brynjar Magnusson

### Affiliation:

Personal

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE diverse_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), is_active BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO diverse_data (name, amount, is_active) VALUES ('Alice', 123.45, TRUE);
INSERT INTO diverse_data (name, amount, is_active) VALUES ('Bob', NULL, FALSE);
COPY (SELECT * FROM diverse_data) TO '/path/to/local/diverse_data_export.csv' WITH (FORMAT CSV, FORCE_QUOTE *);
key_bug_features: COPY TO S3 ignores FILE_SIZE_BYTES setting
